# Code RED source/decompiled export
# Source: docs/script_workflow/DEBUG_CHEAT_MENU_FINDINGS.md
# SHA1: 9c1298f71ca1e27340d78f0afec4f3ac5e71eada

# Code RED Debug / Cheat Menu Findings

## Current conclusion

The built-in cheat menu shell is in `content.rpf` UI XML files. The uploaded DLC RPFs checked in this pass looked like WSI/world/model placement archives, not the actual cheat menu code source.

Strong UI chain found from the resource scan:

```text
content/ui/userinterface.sc.xml
  -> includes Debug.sc
  -> includes pausemenu/PauseMenuScene.sc
  -> options.sc.xml includes Extras.sc.xml
  -> Extras.sc.xml defines rdrExtrasLayer / CheatsList
  -> pausemenuscene.sc.xml handles popup_cheatInput and UI_Cheat* events
```

## Best file leads

```text
root/content/ui/userinterface.sc.xml
root/content/ui/debug.sc.xml
root/content/ui/pausemenu/options.sc.xml
root/content/ui/pausemenu/extras.sc.xml
root/content/ui/pausemenu/pausemenuscene.sc.xml
root/strings/interface_win32.strtbl
root/strings/global_win32.strtbl
root/flash/brplru/pause_main.wsf
root/flash/brplru/list.wsf
root/flash/brplru/popup.wsf
```

## UI behavior clues

`debug.sc.xml` is currently only a shell:

```xml
<uiobject id="DebugMenu"></uiobject>
```

`extras.sc.xml` defines the visible cheat list shell:

```xml
<rdrExtrasLayer id="ExtrasLayer">
  <onshow expr="Enter(CheatsList)"></onshow>
  <UIScrollableList id="CheatsList" ...></UIScrollableList>
</rdrExtrasLayer>
```

`options.sc.xml` routes the player into the extras/cheat UI and calls the native/UI object method:

```xml
<action expr="ExtrasLayer.OnKeyboardActivate()"></action>
<action expr="SendEvent('UI_OpenCheatsMsgBox')"></action>
```

`pausemenuscene.sc.xml` handles the cheat input popups and cheat events:

```xml
SendEvent('UI_CheatEntered')
SendEvent('UI_CheatCancelled')
SendEvent('UI_CheatAccepted')
SendEvent('UI_CheatDeclined')
```

## Important negative result

A scan of `.wsc` and `.sco` scripts did not find normal readable `cheat`, `CheatsList`, `rdrExtrasLayer`, or `UI_Cheat*` string hits.

That means the actual list-filling / unlock / enable code is likely one of these:

1. native UI class code backing `rdrExtrasLayer`
2. game executable or DLL code
3. undecoded Flash/WSF resources
4. script bytecode without readable string labels

Do not assume this is a normal DLC `.wsc` script until a real hit proves it.

## Next no-guess search target

Search the PC executable / DLL folder, if available, for:

```text
rdrExtrasLayer
CheatsList
GameCheat_Label_
GameCheat_Desc_
UI_CheatEntered
UI_OpenCheatsMsgBox
DebugMenu
```

If those strings exist in the executable or a DLL, the cheat menu behavior is probably compiled/native, not archive script logic.
